original work, some of which are the following : (1) Prafnajndna in
70 Arya verses on horary astrology.2 (2) He seems to have written a
work en tit! ed Bha ffo tpala-hord-Sdstram in 75 verses. (3) According to
Al-BlrUnl, Utpala wrote a work entitled Rdhunrdkarana {Rdhunird-
karana ?) and Karanapdta, whose authenticity cannot be vouchsafed.
(4) He states that he has written a work on architecture,
from which he quotes five lines in the Brhat Samhita.3 Al-Biruni's
statements about his authorship of a work called the Sridhava are
conflicting.4
His Contribution : We have already pointed out Utpala's
unique veneration for Varahamihira. He has rendered yeoman
service to the cause of Indian culture and Sanskrit learning by
means of his vast knowledge and practical experience of many
branches of learning. But for his erudition and able exposition many
passages and technical terms in this work would have remained
obscure to the readers. His quotations from many unknown works
